title: Welcome remarks for 'The Evolving Landscape of Bank Funding'
summary: Ceremonial welcome remarks opening the second annual bank-funding conference co-hosted with the Cleveland and Atlanta Feds. Logan emphasizes the importance of diverse perspectives and highlights two funding-policy issues for discussion: modernizing the discount window (including coordination with the Federal Home Loan Banks) and the unlevel playing field in deposit insurance between too-big-to-fail banks and community banks. There is no read on the macro outlook or the stance of monetary policy, so it is non-policy for reaction-function purposes.

key_quotes.quote: The Federal Reserve banks have been streamlining and modernizing discount window operations so we can most efficiently and effectively serve banks when they come to borrow.
key_quotes.context: Operational/funding-policy theme of the conference welcome.

key_quotes.quote: The unlevel playing field between community banks and too-big-to-fail giants undermines the vibrant, competitive banking ecosystem that our economy needs.
key_quotes.context: Deposit-insurance reform discussion, a regulatory rather than monetary-policy topic.
